name: Holiday Cheddar Date Cake
recipeIngredient: 16 ounces Dates; Pitted and Finely Chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 300 degrees F. Grease and flour a 10-inch tube pan.
2. In a large bowl, beat the butter and light brown sugar with an electric mixer on medium until well blended.
3. Add the eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Beat in the shredded cheddar cheese.
4. Sift together the flour, baking soda, salt, ground cinnamon, and ground cloves into a separate bowl.
5. In another bowl, combine the dates, pecans, cherries, and raisins.
6. Add 2 tablespoons of the sifted dry ingredients to the wet mixture, tossing lightly to coat the fruit and nuts.
7. Alternately add the remaining sifted dry ingredients and milk to the butter mixture, beating until well blended.
8. Stir in the coated fruit mixture by hand until evenly distributed throughout the batter.
9. Pour the batter into the prepared pan.
10. Bake for 2 to 2 1/2 hours in the preheated oven, or until the cake pulls away from the side of the pan and springs back when lightly pressed.
11. Let cool for 15 minutes on a wire rack before removing from the pan. Cool completely on the wire rack.
12. Store in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 weeks.
13. To serve, cut into thin slices.
